zh.lifeSummary: 當Roger II de Clifford於1242年之後出生在英國英格蘭賀福爾克利福德時，他的父親Roger de Clifford8779歲，母親Countess Hawise Botterell8785歲。他於1265年6月28日與Isabel Veteripont在英國賀福爾克利福德結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1282年11月6日在威爾士安格萊，埋葬在英國英格蘭恩布利亞沙普聖 瑪麗 馬格達萊也斯 艾比。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Roger II de Clifford于1242年之后出生在英国英格兰賀福爾克利福德时，他的父亲Roger de Clifford8779岁，母亲Countess Hawise Botterell8785岁。他于1265年6月28日与Isabel Veteripont在英国賀福爾克利福德结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他去世于1282年11月6日在威尔士安格萊，埋葬在英国英格兰恩布利亞沙普聖 瑪麗 馬格達萊也斯 艾比。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Clifford
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Irish: habitational name from any of various places called Clifford in Devon, Gloucestershire, and Yorkshire, and in particular Herefordshire. The placename is derived from Old English clif ‘slope’ + ford ‘ford’.

Irish: adoption of the name in 1 above as Anglicization of several Irish names, especially Ó Clúmháin ‘descendant of Clúmhán’, which was the surname of an ecclesiastical family in Sligo and can be traced back to the 12th century. The personal name meant ‘little hairy one’, a diminutive of Irish clúmach ‘hairy’, from clúmh ‘feathers, plumage, down; hair or fur’. Clifford was also adopted for Coleman and in Fermanagh for Crifferty, Clifferty, and Cliffordy, which are Anglicized forms of Mac Raibheartaigh (compare Rafferty ).

History: A powerful Anglo-Norman family of this name in England and Ireland trace their descent from Walter de Clifford, who took the name from Clifford (Castle) in Herefordshire in the 12th century, after acquiring the Clifford barony by marriage.

variants.name: Coleman
meanings: Irish and English: from the Middle English personal name Col(e)man, Old Irish Colmán, earlier Columbán, adopted as Old Norse Kalman. It was introduced into Cumbria, Westmorland, and Yorkshire by Norwegians from Ireland and probably spread widely across England. Ó Colmáin (‘descendant of Colmán’) was the name of an Irish missionary to Europe, also known as Saint Columban(us) (c. 540–615), who founded the monastery of Bobbio in northern Italy in 614. Columbanus is formally a derivative of the Latin for ‘dove’, seen in the name of the 6th-century missionary known in English as Saint Columba (521–597), who converted the Picts to Christianity. This surname is also very common among African Americans.
Irish: from Mac Colmáin or Ó Colmáin ‘son (or descendant) of Colmán’.
Americanized form of Jewish (Ashkenazic)   Kalman   or   Kolman  .

nameMeanings.name: Roger
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French personal name, Rog(i)er, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, from hrōd ‘fame’ + gār, gēr ‘spear’. This was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain, replacing the native Old English form Hrōðgār. Roger was one of the most popular boys' names throughout the medieval period, but less so after the Reformation, though it has continued in regular use to the present day. Roger, Count of Sicily ( c.1031–1101 ), son of Tancred, recovered Sicily from the Arabs. His son, also called Roger, ruled Sicily as king, presiding over a court noted for its splendour and patronage of the arts.

variants.name: Rodger
meanings: Variant spelling of   Roger  , in part from the surname derived from the given name in the Middle Ages.

nameMeanings.name: II
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Violet  ,   Vivien  , or   Vivian .

variants.name: Vivien
meanings: Earlier generally taken as a variant of the boy's name Vivian , but now also rarely used for boys. This spelling was quite common in Old French. Its use as a girl's name in the English-speaking world was influenced by Tennyson's Merlin and Vivien ( 1859 ). This name, from Arthurian legend, may represent an altered form of a Celtic name (perhaps akin to the Irish Gaelic name Béibhinn ‘white lady’, pronounced ‘bee-veen’). The actress Vivien Leigh ( 1913–67 ) was christened Vivian.

variants.name: Violet
meanings: From the name of the flower (Old French violette, Late Latin violetta, a diminutive of viola). This was one of the earliest flower names to become popular in Britain, being used as early as 1700 and becoming well established in the 19th century, although it is now somewhat out of favour.

variants.name: Vivian
meanings: Originally a boy's name, from an Old French form of the Latin name Vivianus (probably a derivative of vivus ‘alive’), but now more frequent as a girl's name. The name was borne by a 5th-century bishop of Saintes in western France, remembered for protecting his people during the invasion of the Visigoths.

en.lifeSummary: When Roger II de Clifford was born after 1242, in Clifford, Herefordshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Roger de Clifford, was 8779 and his mother, Countess Hawise Botterell, was 8785. He married Isabel Veteripont on 28 June 1265, in Clifford, Herefordshire, England. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 2 daughters. He died on 6 November 1282, in Anglesey, Wales, and was buried in St Mary Magdalene's Abbey, Shap, Cumbria, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Roger II de Clifford est né après 1242, à Clifford, Herefordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Roger de Clifford, avait 8779 ans et sa mère, Countess Hawise Botterell, avait 8785 ans. Il a épousé Isabel Veteripont le 28 juin 1265, à Clifford, Herefordshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé le 6 novembre 1282, à Anglesey, Galles, et a été enterré à St Mary Magdalene's Abbey, Shap, Cumbria, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

November 1282 in Anglesey, Wales und wurde in St Mary Magdalene's Abbey, Shap, Cumbria, England, United Kingdom begraben.

ja.lifeSummary: Roger II de Cliffordが1242年以後にイギリスイギリスハーエフォードシュアイアクリフォードで生まれた時，父Roger de Cliffordは8779歳，母Countess Hawise Botterellは8785歳であった。1265年6月28日にイギリスハーエフォードシュアイアクリフォードでIsabel Veteripontと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1282年11月6日にウェールズアングレセイで死去し，イギリスイギリスクウンブリアシャップスト マリー マグダリーネ'ス アビーにて埋葬された。

ko.Shap Abbey, Shap, Cumbria, England: 영국 잉글랜드 쿠브리아 샤프 스트 메리 막그다렌'스 애브브이
ko.lifeSummary: Roger II de Clifford(이)가 1242년 이후 영국 잉글랜드 허퍼드실르 클리퍼드에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Roger de Clifford(은)는 8779세였고, 그의 어머니 Countess Hawise Botterell(은)는 8785세였다. 그는 1265년 6월 28일 영국 허퍼드실르 클리퍼드에서 Isabel Veteripont(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1282년 11월 6일 웨일스 앤글레시에서 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 쿠브리아 샤프 스트 메리 막그다렌'스 애브브이에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Roger II de Clifford nasceu após 1242, em Clifford, Herefordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Roger de Clifford, tinha 8779 anos e sua mãe, Countess Hawise Botterell, tinha 8785 anos. Ele casou-se com Isabel Veteripont em 28 de junho de 1265, em Clifford, Herefordshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 2 filhas. Ele faleceu em 6 de novembro de 1282, em Anglesey, Gales, e foi sepultado em St Mary Magdalene's Abbey, Shap, Cumbria, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.Shap Abbey, Shap, Cumbria, England: ст мэри магдеилине'с эбби, шап, кумбря, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Roger II de Clifford родился после 1242 г. в местности клиффорд, хирефордшир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Roger de Clifford было 8779 лет, а матери, которую звали Countess Hawise Botterell, 8785. Он женился на женщине по имени Isabel Veteripont 28 июня 1265 г. в местности клиффорд, хирефордшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 2 дочери. Он умер 6 ноября 1282 г. в местности англеси, Уэльс и был похоронен в местности ст мэри магдеилине'с эбби, шап, кумбря, Англия, Великобритания.

Se casó con Isabel Veteripont el 28 de junio de 1265, en Clifford, Herefordshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió el 6 de noviembre de 1282, en Isla Oscura, Gales, y fue sepultado en St Mary Magdalene's Abbey, Shap, Cumbria, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
